I'll provide a comprehensive **Reply Draft Generation** system for customer support automation. This assumes previous phases handled ticket ingestion, classification, and sentiment analysis.

## 1. File Structure

```
reply_generator/
├── app/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── main.py                 # FastAPI entry point
```

## 2. Complete Working Code

### Core Configuration (`app/config.py`)

```python
import os
from typing import Optional, List
from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings
from functools import lru_cache


class Settings(BaseSettings):
    # Application
    APP_NAME: str = "ReplyDraftGenerator"
    DEBUG: bool = False
    LOG_LEVEL: str = "INFO"
    
    # Database
    DATABASE_URL: str = "postgresql://user:pass@localhost/replygen"
    REDIS_URL: str = "redis://localhost:6379/0"
    
    # LLM Configuration
    OPENAI_API_KEY: Optional[str] = None
    ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: Optional[str] = None
    DEFAULT_LLM_PROVIDER: str = "openai"  # or "anthropic"
    DEFAULT_MODEL: str = "gpt-4-turbo-preview"
    MAX_TOKENS: int = 1500
    TEMPERATURE: float = 0.7
    
    # Generation Settings
    MAX_CONTEXT_LENGTH: int = 4000  # Characters
    ENABLE_QUALITY_CHECK: bool = True
    REQUIRE_APPROVAL_THRESHOLD: float = 0.8  # Confidence score
    
    # Templates
    TEMPLATE_DIR: str = "templates"
    DEFAULT_TONE: str = "professional"
    ALLOWED_TONES: List[str] = ["professional", "friendly", "formal", "empathetic", "technical"]
    
    # Safety
    MAX_REPLY_LENGTH: int = 3000
    FORBIDDEN_PHRASES: List[str] = ["I don't care", "not my problem", "you're wrong"]
    
    class Config:
        env_file = ".env"


@lru_cache()
def get_settings() -> Settings:
    return Settings()
```

### Data Models (`app/models/schemas.py`)

```python
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Optional, List, Dict, Any, Literal
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, validator
from enum import Enum


class TicketStatus(str, Enum):
    OPEN = "open"
    PENDING = "pending"
    RESOLVED = "resolved"
    ESCALATED = "escalated"


class Priority(str, Enum):
    LOW = "low"
    MEDIUM = "medium"
    HIGH = "high"
    URGENT = "urgent"


class ToneStyle(str, Enum):
    PROFESSIONAL = "professional"
    FRIENDLY = "friendly"
    FORMAL = "formal"
    EMPATHETIC = "empathetic"
    TECHNICAL = "technical"


class CustomerInfo(BaseModel):
    customer_id: str
    name: str
    email: str
    tier: Optional[str] = "standard"
    previous_tickets: int = 0
    sentiment_history: Optional[str] = None


class TicketMessage(BaseModel):
    message_id: str
    author: Literal["customer", "agent", "system"]
    content: str
    timestamp: datetime
    internal_notes: Optional[str] = None


class TicketContext(BaseModel):
    ticket_id: str
    subject: str
    status: TicketStatus
    priority: Priority
    category: Optional[str] = None
    sentiment_score: Optional[float] = Field(None, ge=-1, le=1)
    intent: Optional[str] = None
    customer: CustomerInfo
    conversation_history: List[TicketMessage]
    metadata: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None


class GenerationRequest(BaseModel):
    ticket_context: TicketContext
    tone: ToneStyle = ToneStyle.PROFESSIONAL
    template_id: Optional[str] = None
    custom_instructions: Optional[str] = None
    include_sources: bool = True
    max_length: Optional[int] = Field(None, le=3000)
    
    @validator('tone')
    def validate_tone(cls, v):
        if v not in [t.value for t in ToneStyle]:
            raise ValueError(f"Invalid tone: {v}")
        return v


class GeneratedReply(BaseModel):
    reply_id: str
    ticket_id: str
    subject_line: Optional[str] = None
    body: str
    tone_used: ToneStyle
    confidence_score: float = Field(..., ge=0, le=1)
    generation_method: Literal["ai", "template", "hybrid"]
    suggested_attachments: Optional[List[str]] = None
    warnings: Optional[List[str]] = None
    suggested_follow_ups: Optional[List[str]] = None
    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)
    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = Field(default_factory=dict)


class Template(BaseModel):
    template_id: str
    name: str
    category: str
    tone: ToneStyle
    subject_template: Optional[str]
    body_template: str
    variables: List[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    is_active: bool = True
    usage_count: int = 0


class ReplyFeedback(BaseModel):
    reply_id: str
    was_used: bool
    edits_made: Optional[int] = None  # Levenshtein distance or edit count
    time_to_send: Optional[int] = None  # Seconds
    agent_rating: Optional[int] = Field(None, ge=1, le=5)
    customer_satisfaction: Optional[int] = Field(None, ge=1, le=5)
    feedback_text: Optional[str] = None
```

### Database Models (`app/models/database.py`)

```python
from sqlalchemy import Column, String, Float, DateTime, JSON, Integer, Boolean, Text, create_engine
from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
from sqlalchemy.sql import func
from app.config import get_settings

Base = declarative_base()
settings = get_settings()


class GeneratedReplyDB(Base):
    __tablename__ = "generated_replies"
    
    id = Column(String, primary_key=True)
    ticket_id = Column(String, index=True, nullable=False)
    subject_line = Column(String, nullable=True)
    body = Column(Text, nullable=False)
    tone_used = Column(String, nullable=False)
    confidence_score = Column(Float, nullable=False)
    generation_method = Column(String, nullable=False)
    suggested_attachments = Column(JSON, default=list)
    warnings = Column(JSON, default=list)
    created_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now())
    metadata = Column(JSON, default=dict)
    feedback_rating = Column(Integer, nullable=True)
    was_sent = Column(Boolean, default=False)


class TemplateDB(Base):
    __tablename__ = "templates"
    
    id = Column(String, primary_key=True)
    name = Column(String, nullable=False)
    category = Column(String, index=True, nullable=False)
    tone = Column(String, nullable=False)
    subject_template = Column(String, nullable=True)
    body_template = Column(Text, nullable=False)
    variables = Column(JSON, default=list)
    is_active = Column(Boolean, default=True)
    usage_count = Column(Integer, default=0)
    created_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now())
    updated_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), onupdate=func.now())


# Database initialization
def init_db():
    engine = create_engine(settings.DATABASE_URL)
    Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)
```

### LLM Client (`app/utils/llm_client.py`)

```python
import openai
import anthropic
from typing import AsyncGenerator, Optional, Dict, Any
from app.config import get_settings

settings = get_settings()


class LLMClient:
    def __init__(self):
        self.provider = settings.DEFAULT_LLM_PROVIDER
        self.openai_client = openai.AsyncOpenAI(api_key=settings.OPENAI_API_KEY) if settings.OPENAI_API_KEY else None
        self.anthropic_client = anthropic.AsyncAnthropic(api_key=settings.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) if settings.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY else None
    
    async def generate(
        self,
        prompt: str,
        system_prompt: Optional[str] = None,
        temperature: float = None,
        max_tokens: int = None,
        model: Optional[str] = None
    ) -> str:
        temp = temperature or settings.TEMPERATURE
        tokens = max_tokens or settings.MAX_TOKENS
        model = model or settings.DEFAULT_MODEL
        
        if self.provider == "openai" and self.openai_client:
            return await self._openai_generate(prompt, system_prompt, temp, tokens, model)
        elif self.provider == "anthropic" and self.anthropic_client:
            return await self._anthropic_generate(prompt, system_prompt, temp, tokens)
        else:
            raise ValueError(f"Provider {self.provider} not configured")
    
    async def _openai_generate(
        self,
        prompt: str,
        system_prompt: Optional[str],
        temperature: float,
        max_tokens: int,
        model: str
    ) -> str:
        messages = []
        if system_prompt:
            messages.append({"role": "system", "content": system_prompt})
        messages.append({"role": "user", "content": prompt})
        
        response = await self.openai_client.chat.completions.create(
            model=model,
            messages=messages,
            temperature=temperature,
            max_tokens=max_tokens
        )
        return response.choices[0].message.content
    
    async def _anthropic_generate(
        self,
        prompt: str,
        system_prompt: Optional[str],
        temperature: float,
        max_tokens: int
    ) -> str:
        full_prompt = f"{system_prompt}\n\n{prompt}" if system_prompt else prompt
        
        response = await self.anthropic_client.messages.create(
            model="claude-3-opus-20240229",
            max_tokens=max_tokens,
            temperature=temperature,
            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": full_prompt}]
        )
        return response.content[0].text
    
    async def stream_generate(
        self,
        prompt: str,
        system_prompt: Optional[str] = None
    ) -> AsyncGenerator[str, None]:
        """Stream generation for real-time UI updates"""
        if self.provider == "openai" and self.openai_client:
            messages = []
            if system_prompt:
                messages.append({"role": "system", "content": system_prompt})
            messages.append({"role": "user", "content": prompt})
            
            stream = await self.openai_client.chat.completions.create(
                model=settings.DEFAULT_MODEL,
                messages=messages,
                temperature=settings.TEMPERATURE,
                max_tokens=settings.MAX_TOKENS,
                stream=True
            )
            
            async for chunk in stream:
                if chunk.choices[0].delta.content:
                    yield chunk.choices[0].delta.content


def get_llm_client() -> LLMClient:
    return LLMClient()
```

### Template Engine (`app/services/template_engine.py`)

```python
import json
import re
from typing import List, Optional, Dict, Any
from jinja2 import Environment, FileSystemLoader, Template as JinjaTemplate
from app.models.schemas import Template, TicketContext, ToneStyle
from app.config import get_settings

settings = get_settings()


class TemplateEngine:
    def __init__(self):
        self.env = Environment(loader=FileSystemLoader(settings.TEMPLATE_DIR))
        self.custom_templates: Dict[str, Template] = {}
    
    def load_template(self, template_id: str, context: TicketContext) -> Optional[Template]:
        """Load template from DB or filesystem"""
        # Try database first (simplified - would query SQLAlchemy)
        if template_id in self.custom_templates:
            return self.custom_templates[template_id]
        
        # Try filesystem
        try:
            template = self.env.get_template(f"{template_id}.j2")
            return Template(
                template_id=template_id,
                name=template_id,
                category="general",
                tone=ToneStyle.PROFESSIONAL,
                body_template=template.source,
                variables=self._extract_variables(template.source)
            )
        except Exception:
            return None
    
    def render_template(self, template: Template, context: TicketContext, extra_vars: Dict = None) -> str:
        """Render template with context variables"""
        variables = {
            "customer_name": context.customer.name,
            "customer_tier": context.customer.tier,
            "ticket_id": context.ticket_id,
            "subject": context.subject,
            "category": context.category or "General",
            "priority": context.priority.value,
            "conversation_summary": self._summarize_conversation(context.conversation_history),
            "last_message": context.conversation_history[-1].content if context.conversation_history else "",
            **(extra_vars or {})
        }
        
        jinja_template = JinjaTemplate(template.body_template)
        return jinja_template.render(**variables)
    
    def find_best_template(self, context: TicketContext) -> Optional[Template]:
        """Select best template based on category and tone"""
        # Simplified logic - in production, use embeddings or classification
        if context.category == "billing":
            return self.load_template("billing_inquiry", context)
        elif context.category == "technical":
            return self.load_template("technical_support", context)
        elif context.sentiment_score and context.sentiment_score < -0.5:
            return self.load_template("empathetic_response", context)
        return self.load_template("general_response", context)
    
    def _extract_variables(self, template_str: str) -> List[str]:
        """Extract Jinja2 variables from template"""
        pattern = r'{{\\s*([\\w_]+)\\s*}}'
        return list(set(re.findall(pattern, template_str)))
    
    def _summarize_conversation(self, messages: List[Any]) -> str:
        """Create brief summary of conversation thread"""
        if not messages:
            return ""
        
        customer_msgs = [m.content for m in messages if m.author == "customer"]
        if not customer_msgs:
            return ""
        
        # Return last 2 customer messages as summary
        return " | ".join(customer_msgs[-2:])
    
    def register_template(self, template: Template):
        """Register custom template"""
        self.custom_templates[template.template_id] = template


# Global instance
template_engine = TemplateEngine()
```
